title: Things I Forgot to Mention (Part One)

sentences.en: Sunday. Flipping back through this diary, I realized I forgot to mention a whole bunch of small, random things from this year, so here they are, all at once.
sentences.zh: 星期天。翻回這本日記前面，我發現今年還有一大堆零散的小事忘了寫，所以就一次全部補在這裡。

sentences.en: I never mentioned that our neighbor, Mrs. Huang, is a retired firewoman who still keeps her old helmet on a shelf by her front door.
sentences.zh: 我從沒提過我們鄰居黃太太，她是一位退休的女消防員，到現在還把她的舊頭盔擺在門口的架子上。

sentences.en: I also never mentioned that a real police officer once gave our whole class a brief, friendly talk about basic street safety near school.
sentences.zh: 我也沒提過，有一位真正的警官曾經在學校附近，給我們全班一場簡短又親切的基本街道安全小演講。

sentences.en: There was a foreigner visiting our neighborhood last spring, clearly lost, and William proudly gave him directions that were almost entirely wrong.
sentences.zh: 去年春天有位外國人在我們附近迷路，William 很得意地給了他指路，結果幾乎全部指錯方向。

sentences.en: I forgot to mention that Sophia was once a housewife for many years before she started selling homemade snacks at the local market for extra income.
sentences.zh: 我忘了提，Sophia 曾經當了很多年的家庭主婦，後來才開始在當地市場賣自製小點心，多賺一點收入。

sentences.en: One weekend, a street singer performed near the night market, and Bernice insisted his voice was better than anything on the radio.
sentences.zh: 有個週末，一位街頭歌手在夜市附近表演，Bernice 堅持他的聲音比廣播裡任何一首歌都還好聽。

sentences.en: I never wrote about the time a kind old sir at the bus stop gave William his umbrella during a sudden rainstorm and refused any thanks.
sentences.zh: 我從沒寫過那次，一位親切的老先生在公車站，突然下雨時把他的傘給了 William，還拒絕接受任何道謝。

sentences.en: I also skipped mentioning the school poet who visited our class once, reading a poem so brilliant it made even the noisiest students go completely quiet.
sentences.zh: 我也漏掉提到那位來我們班訪問過一次的校園詩人，他唸的一首詩實在太出色，讓連最吵鬧的學生都安靜了下來。

sentences.en: There was a moment I forgot to record: William dressed up as a cowboy for a school costume day, complete with a plastic toy gun he was very proud of.
sentences.zh: 有個時刻我忘了記下來：William 有一次學校變裝日打扮成牛仔，還配了一把他非常自豪的玩具塑膠槍。

sentences.en: I never described how beautiful the sunset looked one evening, the whole sky glowing a shade of gold that made even William stop running for a full minute.
sentences.zh: 我從沒描述過某天傍晚夕陽有多美，整片天空發著一種金色的光芒，連 William 都因此停止奔跑整整一分鐘。

sentences.en: I forgot to mention Perry's brief, dramatic phase of being deaf to every single command except the word "dinner," which he somehow always heard perfectly.
sentences.zh: 我忘了提 Perry 有一段誇張的時期，對每個指令都像聾了一樣，唯獨「晚餐」這個字，他永遠都能完美聽見。

sentences.en: I never mentioned my brief obsession with a very strict diet plan I found online, which lasted exactly one day before I gave up and ate a whole bowl of noodles.
sentences.zh: 我從沒提過我曾經短暫迷上一份在網路上找到的超嚴格飲食計畫，結果只撐了一天就放棄，吃掉了一整碗麵。

sentences.en: There was a day I completely forgot to write about: a sudden power outage left our whole street dark for two hours, and we ate dinner by candlelight like it was an adventure.
sentences.zh: 有一天我完全忘了寫：一場突然的停電讓我們整條街暗了兩個小時，我們就著燭光吃晚餐，感覺像一場冒險。

sentences.en: I forgot to mention that our class has one classmate who is famously clever at math but somehow cannot spell his own last name correctly half the time.
sentences.zh: 我忘了提，我們班有一位同學數學出了名的聰明，卻有一半的時間連自己的姓都拼不對。

sentences.en: Lesson of this catch-up entry: a whole year holds way more small moments than any diary can ever fully contain, no matter how much I try to write down.
sentences.zh: 這篇補寫日記的心得：一整年裝著的小小片刻，遠比任何一本日記能完整收下的還多，不管我多努力寫下來都一樣。

quiz.question: What did Mrs. Huang used to do before retiring?
What is the one command Perry always seems to hear, even during his "deaf" phase?
What happens during the sudden power outage Coco forgot to write about?
options: The family eats dinner by candlelight like an adventure.
Perry runs away in the dark.
They go to a neighbor's house.
